source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the logging service enabled

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the logging service enabled
input_row.description: Create a logging configuration to forward cluster and app logs to IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA. IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA offers administrators, DevOps teams, and developers advanced features to filter, search, and tail log data, define alerts, and design custom views to monitor application and system logs.
input_row.rationale: Troubleshoot logs in real time to diagnose issues and identify problems. By using the live streaming tail feature, developers and DevOps teams can diagnose issues, analyze stack traces and exceptions, identify the source of errors, and monitor different log sources through a single view. Issue alerts to be notified of important actions. To act promptly on application and services events that you identify as critical or warning, DevOps teams can configure alert notification integrations to the following systems: email, Slack, webHook, and PagerDuty. Export logs to a local file for analysis or to an archive service to meet auditing requirements. Export specific log lines to a local copy or archive logs from IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA to IBM Cloud Object Storage. Log lines are exported in JSON line format. Logs are archived in JSON format and preserve the metadata that is associated with each line. Control logging infrastructure costs by customizing what logs to manage through IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA. Control the cost of your logging infrastructure in the IBM Cloud by configuring the log sources for which you want to collect and manage logs. Impact: Before you provision an instance of IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA, consider the following information: • Log data is hosted on the IBM Cloud. • The IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service is operated by LogDNA. • Your users must have platform permissions to create, view, and delete an instance of a service in the IBM Cloud. • Your users must have platform permissions to create resources within the context of the resource group where you plan to provision the LogDNA instance. After you provision an instance of IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA, an account is created in LogDNA, and you receive the ingestion key for your account. Then, you must configure your log sources. Different pricing plans are available that you can choose for an IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA instance. Each plan defines the number of days that data is retained for search, the number of users allowed to manage the data, and the LogDNA features that are enabled.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon --> Resource list 3. Select your cluster under Clusters to go to the details page. 4. Select the Overview tab 5. Under Logging there should be a button to Launch the LogDNA dashboard. If instead the button is labeled Connect , then logging is not enabled. From Command Line: 1. Install the IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service observability plug-in. ibmcloud plugin install observe-service 2. Check whether a logging configuration is added to your cluster. If one is added, the output of this command will include the logging instance name and ID along with some other information. ibmcloud ob logging config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> Here is example output showing a cluster which has logging enabled ibmcloud ob logging config list --cluster example_cluster_logging_enabled Listing configurations... OK Instance name:      IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA-r9 Instance ID:        eb70d90c-846a-475a-8e9d-577c2c122186 CRN:                crn:v1:bluemix:public:logdna:us- south:a/9c1bf944d2043179dae70822c0168e15:eb70d90c-846a-475a-8e9d- 577c2c122186:: Agent Namespace:    ibm-observe Private Endpoint:   false Discovered Agent:   false
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon --> Resource list 3. Select your cluster under Clusters to go to the details page. 4. Select the Overview tab 5. Under Logging , click Connect . 6. Select the region and the IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service instance that you want to use, and click Connect . From Command Line: 1. Install the IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service observability plug-in. ibmcloud plugin install observe-service 2. Create the LogDNA logging configuration. ibmcloud ob logging config create --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> --instance <LogDNA_instance_name_or_ID> Default Value: By default, logs are generated and written locally for all of the following IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service cluster components: worker nodes, containers, applications, persistent storage, Ingress application load balancer, Kubernetes API, and the kube- system namespace. However, the logging service for collecting, forwarding, and viewing these logs is not enabled for IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters by default. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers-health#logdna 2. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers-observability_cli 3. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/Log-Analysis-with-LogDNA
input_row.impact: Before you provision an instance of IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA, consider the following information: • Log data is hosted on the IBM Cloud. • The IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service is operated by LogDNA. • Your users must have platform permissions to create, view, and delete an instance of a service in the IBM Cloud. • Your users must have platform permissions to create resources within the context of the resource group where you plan to provision the LogDNA instance. After you provision an instance of IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA, an account is created in LogDNA, and you receive the ingestion key for your account. Then, you must configure your log sources. Different pricing plans are available that you can choose for an IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA instance. Each plan defines the number of days that data is retained for search, the number of users allowed to manage the data, and the LogDNA features that are enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control is well-suited for an automated audit approach as it leverages IBM Cloud CLI commands to verify if logging is enabled for IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters. The audit instructions explicitly provide CLI commands (`ibmcloud ob logging config list`) that can be used to check the configuration status of logging. This indicates that all necessary checks can be performed using the IBM Cloud CLI, thus making it feasible to automate.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_service_logging_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud CLI Command:** Use `ibmcloud ob logging config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>` to list logging configurations for a specific cluster.
- **Objective:** Check if a logging configuration exists for each Kubernetes cluster, indicating that logging is enabled.
- **Specific Check:** Verify that the output of the CLI command includes both the logging instance name and ID, which confirms logging is set up.
- **Pass Criteria:** The presence of a valid logging configuration entry, which includes the logging instance name, ID, and CRN.
- **Fail Criteria:** Absence of a logging configuration entry or the presence of a label indicating a need to "Connect," implying logging is not enabled.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Inspect each IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service cluster configured under the user's account.

This approach leverages the ability of IBM Cloud APIs and CLI to access detailed configurations, offering a comprehensive check without requiring manual inspection.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of IBM Cloud CLI commands to verify logging configurations for IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters is appropriate and feasible. The CLI command `ibmcloud ob logging config list` is suitable for automating the check for logging configurations.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are technically correct. The use of `ibmcloud ob logging config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>` is valid for listing logging configurations.
- The pass and fail criteria are clear and appropriate.
- The program name `ibm_kubernetes_service_logging_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity.
- The CLI command is correctly identified, but the description could include more details about handling potential command errors or edge cases.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be more descriptive. Consider `ibm_kubernetes_service_logging_configured` to better reflect the check's focus on configuration status.
- Include error handling and edge case considerations, such as what happens if the CLI command fails or if there are network issues.
- Consider using IBM Cloud API methods as an alternative to the CLI for environments where API integration is preferred or where CLI access is restricted.
- Mention the need to ensure the IBM Cloud CLI is authenticated and configured correctly before running the commands.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and uses appropriate IBM Cloud CLI commands. The automation approach is sound, and the criteria for passing and failing are clear and logical.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Improve the program name to `ibm_kubernetes_service_logging_configured` for better clarity. Add considerations for error handling and potential edge cases in the automation details.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the IBM Cloud CLI provides a direct, scriptable method (`ibmcloud ob logging config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>`) to verify whether logging is enabled for IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters. The audit steps and remediation instructions explicitly reference this CLI capability, confirming that all necessary configuration data is exposed via IBM Cloud APIs and can be reliably checked without manual console inspection.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
ibm_kubernetes_service_cluster_logging_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) clusters and IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA
- API/CLI Method: `ibmcloud ob logging config list --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>`
- Properties to Check: Presence of a logging configuration entry for each cluster, including logging instance name, ID, and CRN in the command output.
- Pass Criteria: The cluster has a valid logging configuration entry (logging instance name, ID, and CRN are present), indicating logging is enabled and connected to LogDNA.
- Fail Criteria: No logging configuration entry is present for the cluster, or the output indicates the need to "Connect" (i.e., logging is not enabled).

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported automation. The final program name was made more descriptive per the second opinion’s suggestion.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
